title: diversifier_index stored as TEXT decimal u128 to avoid SQLite i64 truncation
description: db.rs accounts table stores diversifier_index as TEXT (decimal u128), not INTEGER. The comment explains: SQLite INTEGER is signed 64-bit; a u128 diversifier index exceeding i64::MAX would silently wrap to a negative value in SQLite, corrupting the index. ZIP-32 diversifiers are 11 bytes = 88 bits wide (max 2^88-1), which exceeds i64::MAX (2^63-1). Storing as decimal TEXT avoids this. advance_diversifier_to correctly handles lexicographic vs numeric ordering by doing the comparison in Rust. A contributor might suggest switching to INTEGER 'for efficiency'; that would silently corrupt diversifier indices above 2^63-1, causing address reuse.
close_reason: Observation, no action: TEXT decimal u128 pattern and its rationale are correctly documented in code.
